channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. B
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review B
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org

item.title: Enhanced Andreev reflection in flat-band systems: Wave packet dynamics, dc transport, and the Josephson effect
item.description:
Author(s): Sarbajit Mazumdar, Anamitra Mukherjee, Kush Saha, and Sourin Das

We investigate Andreev reflection (AR) in a proximity-induced normal-superconductor (NS) junction within the extended $α–{\mathcal{T}}_{3}$ lattice, emphasizing the impact of flat bands on AR. Our findings reveal that flat bands significantly enhance AR. Through wave-packet dynamics, we track the re…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134517] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sarbajit Mazumdar, Anamitra Mukherjee, Kush Saha, and Sourin Das

We investigate Andreev reflection (AR) in a proximity-induced normal-superconductor (NS) junction within the extended α–T3 lattice, emphasizing the impact of flat bands on AR. Our findings reveal that flat bands significantly enhance AR. Through wave-packet dynamics, we track the real-time evolution…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134517] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026
item.dc:title: Enhanced Andreev reflection in flat-band systems: Wave packet dynamics, dc transport, and the Josephson effect
item.dc:creator: Sarbajit Mazumdar, Anamitra Mukherjee, Kush Saha, and Sourin Das

item.title: Worldline deconfinement and emergent long-range interaction in the entanglement Hamiltonian and in the entanglement spectrum
item.description:
Author(s): Zenan Liu, Zhe Wang, Dao-Xin Yao, and Zheng Yan

The entanglement spectrum (ES) is a powerful tool for probing topological phases. While its behavior in gapped systems is well understood, its properties in gapless regimes remain unclear. In this work, we employ a quantum Monte Carlo method to study the ES of a two-dimensional square-octagon lattic…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144425] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zenan Liu, Zhe Wang, Dao-Xin Yao, and Zheng Yan

The entanglement spectrum (ES) is a powerful tool for probing topological phases. While its behavior in gapped systems is well understood, its properties in gapless regimes remain unclear. In this work, we employ a quantum Monte Carlo method to study the ES of a two-dimensional square-octagon lattic…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144425] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026
item.dc:title: Worldline deconfinement and emergent long-range interaction in the entanglement Hamiltonian and in the entanglement spectrum

item.title: Interplay of magnetism and topological band robustness in layered rare-earth diantimonides
item.description:
Author(s): Zhian Xu, Ze Yan, Jian Yuan, Kaige Wu, Wei Xia, Xia Wang, Chuanying Xi, Li Pi, Yuzhou Zhao, Shihao Zhang, and Yanfeng Guo

Layered compounds $R\mathrm{S}{\mathrm{b}}_{2}$ ($R$ = light rare earth), which first hosted the discovery of non-rivial topological fermions in nonmagnetic $\mathrm{La}\mathrm{S}{\mathrm{b}}_{2}$, offer an exceptional platform for probing the interplay between magnetism and topological electronic s…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155135] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhian Xu, Ze Yan, Jian Yuan, Kaige Wu, Wei Xia, Xia Wang, Chuanying Xi, Li Pi, Yuzhou Zhao, Shihao Zhang, and Yanfeng Guo

Layered compounds RSb2 (R = light rare earth), which first hosted the discovery of non-rivial topological fermions in nonmagnetic LaSb2, offer an exceptional platform for probing the interplay between magnetism and topological electronic states. Through high-field magnetotransport measurements and f…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155135] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026
item.dc:title: Interplay of magnetism and topological band robustness in layered rare-earth diantimonides
item.dc:creator: Zhian Xu, Ze Yan, Jian Yuan, Kaige Wu, Wei Xia, Xia Wang, Chuanying Xi, Li Pi, Yuzhou Zhao, Shihao Zhang, and Yanfeng Guo

item.title: Various electronic crystal phases in rhombohedral graphene multilayers
item.description:
Author(s): Wangqian Miao and Chu Li

We systematically investigate the emergence of electron crystal phases in rhombohedral multilayer graphene using comprehensive self-consistent Hartree Fock calculations combined with ab initio tight binding model. As the carrier density increases, we uncover an isospin cascade sequence of phase tran…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155136] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Wangqian Miao and Chu Li

We systematically investigate the emergence of electron crystal phases in rhombohedral multilayer graphene using comprehensive self-consistent Hartree Fock calculations combined with ab initio tight binding model. As the carrier density increases, we uncover an isospin cascade sequence of phase tran…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155136] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026

item.title: Laser-induced commensurate-incommensurate transition of charge order in a Hubbard superlattice
item.description:
Author(s): Hua Chai, Zhenyu Cheng, Qinxin Hu, Zhongbing Huang, Xiang Hu, Xuedong Tian, and Liang Du

We investigate the nonequilibrium dynamics of charge density waves in a pumped one-dimensional Hubbard superlattice with staggered on-site Coulomb interactions at half filling, using time-dependent exact diagonalization. In equilibrium, the system exhibits commensurate charge correlations consistent…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165134] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hua Chai, Zhenyu Cheng, Qinxin Hu, Zhongbing Huang, Xiang Hu, Xuedong Tian, and Liang Du

We investigate the nonequilibrium dynamics of charge density waves in a pumped one-dimensional Hubbard superlattice with staggered on-site Coulomb interactions at half filling, using time-dependent exact diagonalization. In equilibrium, the system exhibits commensurate charge correlations consistent…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165134] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026

item.title: Hund-projected Kanamori model: An effective description of Hund's metals near the Mott insulating regime
item.description:
Author(s): Johan Carlström

Hund's coupling plays a decisive role in shaping electron correlations of multi-orbital systems, giving rise to a class of materials—Hund's metals—that combine local-moment physics with metallic transport. Here, we derive an effective low-energy description of such a system near the Mott insulating …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165135] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Johan Carlström

Hund's coupling plays a decisive role in shaping electron correlations of multi-orbital systems, giving rise to a class of materials—Hund's metals—that combine local-moment physics with metallic transport. Here, we derive an effective low-energy description of such a system near the Mott insulating …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165135] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026

item.title: Confinement-induced Majorana modes in a nodal topological superconductor
item.description:
Author(s): Simone Traverso, Niccolò Traverso Ziani, Maura Sassetti, and Fernando Dominguez

We investigate the topological phase diagram of an extension of the Haldane model with equal spin pairing superconductivity. In two dimensions, we find a topological nodal superconducting phase, which exhibits a chiral Majorana mode propagating along the edges of nanoribbons with cylindrical boundar…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165416] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Simone Traverso, Niccolò Traverso Ziani, Maura Sassetti, and Fernando Dominguez

We investigate the topological phase diagram of an extension of the Haldane model with equal spin pairing superconductivity. In two dimensions, we find a topological nodal superconducting phase, which exhibits a chiral Majorana mode propagating along the edges of nanoribbons with cylindrical boundar…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165416] Published Fri Apr 17, 2026

item.title: Magnetic penetration depth in topological superconductors: Effect of Majorana surface states and application for ${\mathrm{UTe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Kazuki Akuzawa, Jushin Tei, Ryoi Ohashi, Satoshi Fujimoto, and Takeshi Mizushima

In this study, we examine how orbital degrees of freedom and Majorana surface states influence the magnetic penetration depth in the superconductor ${\mathrm{UTe}}_{2}$. Using a two-orbital model, we analyze pairing states belonging to the irreducible representations of the ${D}_{2h}$ crystal symmet…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144506] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kazuki Akuzawa, Jushin Tei, Ryoi Ohashi, Satoshi Fujimoto, and Takeshi Mizushima

In this study, we examine how orbital degrees of freedom and Majorana surface states influence the magnetic penetration depth in the superconductor UTe2. Using a two-orbital model, we analyze pairing states belonging to the irreducible representations of the D2h crystal symmetry: Au, B1u, B2u, and B…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144506] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnetic penetration depth in topological superconductors: Effect of Majorana surface states and application for ${\mathrm{UTe}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Kazuki Akuzawa, Jushin Tei, Ryoi Ohashi, Satoshi Fujimoto, and Takeshi Mizushima

item.title: Band-structure picture for topology in strongly correlated systems with the ghost Gutzwiller ansatz
item.description:
Author(s): Ivan Pasqua, Antonio Maria Tagliente, Gabriele Bellomia, Bartomeu Monserrat, Michele Fabrizio, and Carlos Mejuto-Zaera

Understanding the interplay between electronic correlations and band topology remains a central challenge in condensed matter physics, primarily hindered by a language-mismatch problem. While band topology is naturally formulated within a single-particle band theory, strong correlations typically el…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155133] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ivan Pasqua, Antonio Maria Tagliente, Gabriele Bellomia, Bartomeu Monserrat, Michele Fabrizio, and Carlos Mejuto-Zaera

Understanding the interplay between electronic correlations and band topology remains a central challenge in condensed matter physics, primarily hindered by a language-mismatch problem. While band topology is naturally formulated within a single-particle band theory, strong correlations typically el…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155133] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026

item.title: Topological control of corner and edge states in an altermagnetic ${\mathrm{Fe}}_{2}{\mathrm{Se}}_{2}\mathrm{O}$ monolayer
item.description:
Author(s): Yilin Zhang, Zhiqi Chen, Xiaorong Zou, Baibiao Huang, Ying Dai, and Chengwang Niu

Discoveries of both altermagnets and topological states have profoundly reshaped our understanding of condensed-matter physics and materials. However, tunable topological states in altermagnets remain largely unexplored. Here, we put forward the realization of second-order topological insulator (SOT…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155428] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026

Discoveries of both altermagnets and topological states have profoundly reshaped our understanding of condensed-matter physics and materials. However, tunable topological states in altermagnets remain largely unexplored. Here, we put forward the realization of second-order topological insulator (SOT…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155428] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026

item.title: Proximity-induced superconductivity and emerging topological phases in altermagnet-based heterostructures
item.description:
Author(s): Ohidul Alam, Amartya Pal, Paramita Dutta, and Arijit Saha

We present a theoretical framework for investigating superconducting proximity effect in altermagnet (AM)–superconductor (SC) heterostructures. In general, AMs, characterized by vanishing net magnetization but spin-split electronic spectra, provide a promising platform for realizing unconventional q…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155429] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ohidul Alam, Amartya Pal, Paramita Dutta, and Arijit Saha

We present a theoretical framework for investigating superconducting proximity effect in altermagnet (AM)–superconductor (SC) heterostructures. In general, AMs, characterized by vanishing net magnetization but spin-split electronic spectra, provide a promising platform for realizing unconventional q…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155429] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Proximity-induced superconductivity and emerging topological phases in altermagnet-based heterostructures

item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Multichannel Kondo effect in one-dimensional superconducting leads
item.description:
Author(s): Pradip Kattel, Abay Zhakenov, and Natan Andrei

The authors uncover here a rich phase diagram when a quantum spin is placed in an interacting superconducting system, with different phases arising due to the competition of the Kondo and superconducting interactions. Particularly surprising is the appearance of multichannel physics in a gapped superconducting phase. They further show that in each phase, the Hilbert space splits into several towers of excitations. By computing the impurity entropy explicitly in each phase, they show that the excitation towers induce interesting nonmonotonic behavior, which is experimentally accessible.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165130] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pradip Kattel, Abay Zhakenov, and Natan Andrei

The authors uncover here a rich phase diagram when a quantum spin is placed in an interacting superconducting system, with different phases arising due to the competition of the Kondo and superconducting interactions. Particularly surprising is the appearance of multichannel physics in a gapped superconducting phase. They further show that in each phase, the Hilbert space splits into several towers of excitations. By computing the impurity entropy explicitly in each phase, they show that the excitation towers induce interesting nonmonotonic behavior, which is experimentally accessible.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165130] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Multichannel Kondo effect in one-dimensional superconducting leads
item.dc:creator: Pradip Kattel, Abay Zhakenov, and Natan Andrei
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Connection between negative charge transfer and reduced on-site Coulomb energy in the correlated topological metal ${\mathrm{CoTe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): A. R. Shelke, C.-W. Chuang, S. Hamamoto, M. Oura, M. Yoshimura, N. Hiraoka, C.-N. Kuo, C.-S. Lue, A. Fujimori, and A. Chainani

The layered $3d$ transition metal dichalcogenide ${\mathrm{CoTe}}_{2}$ is a topological Dirac type-II metal. However, the Co $3d$-bands in ${\mathrm{CoTe}}_{2}$ do not exhibit the expected correlation-induced band narrowing seen in CoO. We address this conundrum by studying the electronic structure …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165132] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): A. R. Shelke, C.-W. Chuang, S. Hamamoto, M. Oura, M. Yoshimura, N. Hiraoka, C.-N. Kuo, C.-S. Lue, A. Fujimori, and A. Chainani

The layered 3d transition metal dichalcogenide CoTe2 is a topological Dirac type-II metal. However, the Co 3d-bands in CoTe2 do not exhibit the expected correlation-induced band narrowing seen in CoO. We address this conundrum by studying the electronic structure of CoTe2 using hard x-ray photoemiss…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165132] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Connection between negative charge transfer and reduced on-site Coulomb energy in the correlated topological metal ${\mathrm{CoTe}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: A. R. Shelke, C.-W. Chuang, S. Hamamoto, M. Oura, M. Yoshimura, N. Hiraoka, C.-N. Kuo, C.-S. Lue, A. Fujimori, and A. Chainani

item.title: Role of on-site Coulomb energy and negative charge transfer in the Dirac semimetal ${\mathrm{NiTe}}_{2}$

Angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy combined with band structure calculations have shown that the layered transition metal dichalcogenide ${\mathrm{NiTe}}_{2}$ is a type-II Dirac semimetal. However, conflicting conclusions were reported regarding the role of electron correlations in ${\mathrm{…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165133] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): A. R. Shelke, C.-W. Chuang, S. Hamamoto, M. Oura, M. Yoshimura, N. Hiraoka, C.-N. Kuo, C.-S. Lue, A. Fujimori, and A. Chainani

Angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy combined with band structure calculations have shown that the layered transition metal dichalcogenide NiTe2 is a type-II Dirac semimetal. However, conflicting conclusions were reported regarding the role of electron correlations in NiTe2. We study core level…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165133] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Role of on-site Coulomb energy and negative charge transfer in the Dirac semimetal ${\mathrm{NiTe}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: A. R. Shelke, C.-W. Chuang, S. Hamamoto, M. Oura, M. Yoshimura, N. Hiraoka, C.-N. Kuo, C.-S. Lue, A. Fujimori, and A. Chainani
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Spin-spiral instability of the Nagaoka ferromagnet in the crossover between square and triangular lattices
item.description:
Author(s): Darren Pereira and Erich J. Mueller

The hard-core Fermi-Hubbard model displays a massive spin degeneracy at half-filling: superexchange is forbidden in the hard-core limit, so all spin configurations have the same energy. Introducing a single hole, whose kinetic energy depends on the spin state, leads to magnetic ordering. This ordering is lattice dependent. The authors study here how it evolves as one interpolates between a square and triangular lattice, which can be explored using optical lattices. Using perturbative and variational arguments, they identify a phase transition between a ferromagnet and a spin-spiral state.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161114] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Darren Pereira and Erich J. Mueller

The hard-core Fermi-Hubbard model displays a massive spin degeneracy at half-filling: superexchange is forbidden in the hard-core limit, so all spin configurations have the same energy. Introducing a single hole, whose kinetic energy depends on the spin state, leads to magnetic ordering. This ordering is lattice dependent. The authors study here how it evolves as one interpolates between a square and triangular lattice, which can be explored using optical lattices. Using perturbative and variational arguments, they identify a phase transition between a ferromagnet and a spin-spiral state.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L161114] Published Thu Apr 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin-spiral instability of the Nagaoka ferromagnet in the crossover between square and triangular lattices
item.dc:creator: Darren Pereira and Erich J. Mueller
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Generalized Hellmann-Feynman perturbation theory: Applications to topological magnons
item.description:
Author(s): Qi-Hui Chen, Fei-Jie Huang, and Yong-Ping Fu

Conventional perturbation theory, developed for Hermitian matrices, is inadequate for determining the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of bosonic Bogoliubov–de Gennes (BdG) Hamiltonians, whose dynamical structure leads to a non-Hermitian generalized eigenvalue problem. Here we extend the Hellmann-Feynma…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134424] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Qi-Hui Chen, Fei-Jie Huang, and Yong-Ping Fu

Conventional perturbation theory, developed for Hermitian matrices, is inadequate for determining the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of bosonic Bogoliubov–de Gennes (BdG) Hamiltonians, whose dynamical structure leads to a non-Hermitian generalized eigenvalue problem. Here we extend the Hellmann-Feynma…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134424] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.dc:title: Generalized Hellmann-Feynman perturbation theory: Applications to topological magnons

item.title: Interplay of charge density waves, superconductivity, and band topology in a kagome boron monolayer decorated by dialkaline earth metals
item.description:
Author(s): Hao-lin Song, Meng-hui Wang, Guang-ren Na, Zhong-hua Cui, and Lei Wang

The interplay of collective electronic orders represents a central theme in condensed matter physics, and kagome lattices offer fertile ground for studying their competition and coexistence. Although metal-decorated two-dimensional boron kagome lattices can be stabilized, their quantum phases remain…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134515] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026

The interplay of collective electronic orders represents a central theme in condensed matter physics, and kagome lattices offer fertile ground for studying their competition and coexistence. Although metal-decorated two-dimensional boron kagome lattices can be stabilized, their quantum phases remain…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134515] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026

item.title: Superconductivity and geometric superfluid weight of a tunable flat band system
item.description:
Author(s): M. A. Mojarro and Sergio E. Ulloa

We study superconductivity and superfluid weight of the two-dimensional $α\text{−}{\mathcal{T}}_{3}$ lattice with on-site asymmetries, hosting an isolated quasiflat band with tunable bandwidth via a parameter $α$. Within a mean-field approximation of the attractive Hubbard model, we obtain the super…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134516] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): M. A. Mojarro and Sergio E. Ulloa

We study superconductivity and superfluid weight of the two-dimensional α−T3 lattice with on-site asymmetries, hosting an isolated quasiflat band with tunable bandwidth via a parameter α. Within a mean-field approximation of the attractive Hubbard model, we obtain the superconducting order parameter…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134516] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026

item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity
item.prism:section: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Photocurrents induced by $k$-linear terms in semiconductors and semimetals
item.description:
Author(s): M. M. Glazov and E. L. Ivchenko

The authors have developed a six-band k·p model of chiral (1, 1/2)-fermions in semimetals of the T cubic point group symmetry. Here, the model is used to describe the electronic structure, optical absorption, and photocurrent in chiral multifold silicides like RhSi. The effective Hamiltonian incorporates spin-orbit coupling between the orbital momentum L = 1 and spin 1/2 as well as the linear-in-wavevector Rashba term, which is essential for the circular photogalvanic effect. Parameterized using tight-binding calculations, the model accurately reproduces the low-energy band structure. The authors also compute the intersubband absorption spectrum and the circular photocurrent, demonstrating a quantized low-frequency response governed by a topological charge of four and revealing deviations from the universal value with increasing the light frequency.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 139602] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): M. M. Glazov and E. L. Ivchenko

The authors have developed a six-band k·p model of chiral (1, 1/2)-fermions in semimetals of the T cubic point group symmetry. Here, the model is used to describe the electronic structure, optical absorption, and photocurrent in chiral multifold silicides like RhSi. The effective Hamiltonian incorporates spin-orbit coupling between the orbital momentum L = 1 and spin 1/2 as well as the linear-in-wavevector Rashba term, which is essential for the circular photogalvanic effect. Parameterized using tight-binding calculations, the model accurately reproduces the low-energy band structure. The authors also compute the intersubband absorption spectrum and the circular photocurrent, demonstrating a quantized low-frequency response governed by a topological charge of four and revealing deviations from the universal value with increasing the light frequency.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 139602] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.dc:title: Photocurrents induced by $k$-linear terms in semiconductors and semimetals
item.dc:creator: M. M. Glazov and E. L. Ivchenko
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Technical Perspective
item.prism:section: Technical Perspective

item.title: Fluctuation corrections to the free energy of strongly correlated electron systems
item.description:
Author(s): David Riegler, Jannis Seufert, Ronny Thomale, and Peter Wölfle

We determine the free energy of strongly correlated electron systems in the example of the Hubbard model by calculating the contribution of spin and charge fluctuations to the Gutzwiller approximation mean-field result. We employ the slave-boson formulation of Kotliar and Ruckenstein in its spin-rot…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155131] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): David Riegler, Jannis Seufert, Ronny Thomale, and Peter Wölfle

We determine the free energy of strongly correlated electron systems in the example of the Hubbard model by calculating the contribution of spin and charge fluctuations to the Gutzwiller approximation mean-field result. We employ the slave-boson formulation of Kotliar and Ruckenstein in its spin-rot…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155131] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.dc:title: Fluctuation corrections to the free energy of strongly correlated electron systems
item.dc:creator: David Riegler, Jannis Seufert, Ronny Thomale, and Peter Wölfle
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Chern junctions in moiré-patterned graphene/${\mathrm{PbI}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Yan Sun, M. Monteverde, V. Derkach, K. Watanabe, T. Taniguchi, F. Chiodi, H. Bouchiat, and A. D. Chepelianskii

Here, the authors report dissipationless transport at the charge neutrality point, robust fractional conductance plateaux, and quantum interference phenomena in a BN/graphene/PbI2 moiré heterostructure. These effects arise from Chern junctions between domains with distinct Chern numbers, establishing a new device architecture for exploring correlated and topological quantum phases. They show that in this architecture, ballistic channels can survive the transition between integer quantum Hall effect plateaus, something which was not believed to be possible.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155425] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yan Sun, M. Monteverde, V. Derkach, K. Watanabe, T. Taniguchi, F. Chiodi, H. Bouchiat, and A. D. Chepelianskii

Here, the authors report dissipationless transport at the charge neutrality point, robust fractional conductance plateaux, and quantum interference phenomena in a BN/graphene/PbI2 moiré heterostructure. These effects arise from Chern junctions between domains with distinct Chern numbers, establishing a new device architecture for exploring correlated and topological quantum phases. They show that in this architecture, ballistic channels can survive the transition between integer quantum Hall effect plateaus, something which was not believed to be possible.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155425] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.dc:title: Chern junctions in moiré-patterned graphene/${\mathrm{PbI}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Yan Sun, M. Monteverde, V. Derkach, K. Watanabe, T. Taniguchi, F. Chiodi, H. Bouchiat, and A. D. Chepelianskii

item.title: Magnetic and transport properties of single-crystalline TbRhPb: A candidate topological material with a quasikagome lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Rajesh Swami, U. B. Paramanik, and Z. Hossain

We present a comprehensive study of the magnetic, thermodynamic, and transport properties of single-crystalline TbRhPb, a geometrically frustrated rare-earth compound that crystallizes in the noncentrosymmetric ZrNiAl-type structure, where ${\mathrm{Tb}}^{3+}$ ions form a quasikagome arrangement in …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165125] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Rajesh Swami, U. B. Paramanik, and Z. Hossain

We present a comprehensive study of the magnetic, thermodynamic, and transport properties of single-crystalline TbRhPb, a geometrically frustrated rare-earth compound that crystallizes in the noncentrosymmetric ZrNiAl-type structure, where Tb3+ ions form a quasikagome arrangement in the ab plane. Te…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165125] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnetic and transport properties of single-crystalline TbRhPb: A candidate topological material with a quasikagome lattice
item.dc:creator: Rajesh Swami, U. B. Paramanik, and Z. Hossain

item.title: Emergent quantum valley Hall insulator from electron interactions in transition-metal dichalcogenide heterobilayers
item.description:
Author(s): Palash Saha and Michał Zegrodnik

We explore the emergence of topological phases in moiré ${\mathrm{MoTe}}_{2}/{\mathrm{WSe}}_{2}$ bilayer, highlighting the crucial role of spin-orbit coupling and Coulomb interactions at two holes per moiré unit cell $v=2$. Our analysis uncovers robust quantum valley Hall insulating phase and reveal…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165413] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Palash Saha and Michał Zegrodnik

We explore the emergence of topological phases in moiré MoTe2/WSe2 bilayer, highlighting the crucial role of spin-orbit coupling and Coulomb interactions at two holes per moiré unit cell v=2. Our analysis uncovers robust quantum valley Hall insulating phase and reveals that long-range interactions a…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 165413] Published Wed Apr 15, 2026
item.dc:title: Emergent quantum valley Hall insulator from electron interactions in transition-metal dichalcogenide heterobilayers
item.dc:creator: Palash Saha and Michał Zegrodnik

item.title: Symmetry and topology of monitored quantum dynamics
item.description:
Author(s): Zhenyu Xiao and Kohei Kawabata

The interplay between unitary dynamics and quantum measurements induces diverse phenomena in open quantum systems with no counterparts in closed quantum systems at equilibrium. Here, we generally classify Kraus operators and their effective non-Hermitian dynamical generators, thereby establishing th…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134307] Published Tue Apr 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhenyu Xiao and Kohei Kawabata

The interplay between unitary dynamics and quantum measurements induces diverse phenomena in open quantum systems with no counterparts in closed quantum systems at equilibrium. Here, we generally classify Kraus operators and their effective non-Hermitian dynamical generators, thereby establishing th…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134307] Published Tue Apr 14, 2026
item.dc:title: Symmetry and topology of monitored quantum dynamics
item.dc:creator: Zhenyu Xiao and Kohei Kawabata
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Dynamics, dynamical systems, lattice effects
item.prism:section: Dynamics, dynamical systems, lattice effects

item.title: Highly tunable Kondo effect with competing magnetism in the kagome lattice compound ${\mathrm{CsCr}}_{6}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{6}$
item.description:
Author(s): Guofei Yang, Hengrui Gui, Boqin Song, Huiqing Ye, Yifan Wan, Guoqing Zeng, Chengwei Wang, Xudong Zhao, Zekai Shi, Michael Smidman, Chenchao Xu, Chao Cao, Tianping Ying, Lin Jiao, and Huiqiu Yuan

Frustration-induced flat band systems offer a unique pathway to realize Kondo physics. Here, the authors use scanning tunneling spectroscopy and thermodynamic probes to reveal highly tunable Kondo effect in the kagome compound CsCr6Sb6. They show that temperature and in-plane magnetic field tune the competition between Kondo effect and underlying magnetism, establishing this compound as a new paradigm for studying correlated quantum states in 3d electron systems.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155128] Published Tue Apr 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Guofei Yang, Hengrui Gui, Boqin Song, Huiqing Ye, Yifan Wan, Guoqing Zeng, Chengwei Wang, Xudong Zhao, Zekai Shi, Michael Smidman, Chenchao Xu, Chao Cao, Tianping Ying, Lin Jiao, and Huiqiu Yuan

Frustration-induced flat band systems offer a unique pathway to realize Kondo physics. Here, the authors use scanning tunneling spectroscopy and thermodynamic probes to reveal highly tunable Kondo effect in the kagome compound CsCr6Sb6. They show that temperature and in-plane magnetic field tune the competition between Kondo effect and underlying magnetism, establishing this compound as a new paradigm for studying correlated quantum states in 3d electron systems.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155128] Published Tue Apr 14, 2026
item.dc:title: Highly tunable Kondo effect with competing magnetism in the kagome lattice compound ${\mathrm{CsCr}}_{6}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{6}$
item.dc:creator: Guofei Yang, Hengrui Gui, Boqin Song, Huiqing Ye, Yifan Wan, Guoqing Zeng, Chengwei Wang, Xudong Zhao, Zekai Shi, Michael Smidman, Chenchao Xu, Chao Cao, Tianping Ying, Lin Jiao, and Huiqiu Yuan

item.title: Orbital-driven topological phase transition and planar Hall response in ternary telluride Weyl semimetals
item.description:
Author(s): Banasree Sadhukhan and Tanay Nag

We study electronic properties of the ternary tellurides ${\mathrm{TaXTe}}_{4}$ (X = Rh, Ir) using density functional theory and investigate chiral anomaly mediated planar Hall response from ab initio calculations. We show that ${\mathrm{TaRhTe}}_{4}$ is a hybrid Weyl semimetal (WSM), hosting Weyl p…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155130] Published Tue Apr 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Banasree Sadhukhan and Tanay Nag

We study electronic properties of the ternary tellurides TaXTe4 (X = Rh, Ir) using density functional theory and investigate chiral anomaly mediated planar Hall response from ab initio calculations. We show that TaRhTe4 is a hybrid Weyl semimetal (WSM), hosting Weyl points (WPs) of both type-I, type…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155130] Published Tue Apr 14, 2026
item.dc:title: Orbital-driven topological phase transition and planar Hall response in ternary telluride Weyl semimetals
item.dc:creator: Banasree Sadhukhan and Tanay Nag

item.title: Spin flop and slowing of magnetic fluctuations probed by magnetization reversal and magnetic susceptibility in a CrSBr crystal
item.description:
Author(s): M. V. Bakhmetiev, D. L. Gusenkov, A. I. Chernov, and R. B. Morgunov

Changes in magnetic dynamical characteristics accompanying slowing of spin relaxation below 100 K and freezing of spins detected by muon and neutron diffractions have been found. Magnetic phases identified in CrSBr single crystals by slow magnetization relaxation are caused by stepwise changes in ma…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134419] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): M. V. Bakhmetiev, D. L. Gusenkov, A. I. Chernov, and R. B. Morgunov

Changes in magnetic dynamical characteristics accompanying slowing of spin relaxation below 100 K and freezing of spins detected by muon and neutron diffractions have been found. Magnetic phases identified in CrSBr single crystals by slow magnetization relaxation are caused by stepwise changes in ma…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134419] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026

item.title: Phase diagram and excitation spectra of the anisotropic Kitaev-Heisenberg model
item.description:
Author(s): Si-Qi Hou, Wei Wang, Shun-Li Yu, and Jian-Xin Li

The phase diagram and spin excitation spectra of the Kitaev-Heisenberg model with bond anisotropy are investigated using the exact diagonalization algorithm and spin cluster perturbation theory. By adjusting the model parameters and anisotropy intensity, we comprehensively map the complete phase dia…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134421] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Si-Qi Hou, Wei Wang, Shun-Li Yu, and Jian-Xin Li

The phase diagram and spin excitation spectra of the Kitaev-Heisenberg model with bond anisotropy are investigated using the exact diagonalization algorithm and spin cluster perturbation theory. By adjusting the model parameters and anisotropy intensity, we comprehensively map the complete phase dia…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 134421] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026

item.title: Fractional magnetization plateaus and nontrivial magnetic ground state in the kagome antiferromagnet $\mathrm{C}{\mathrm{e}}_{3}\mathrm{MgB}{\mathrm{i}}_{5}$
item.description:
Author(s): Xin Han, Yiqing Hao, Jianfeng Zhang, Zhongchen Xu, Jingyu Yao, Yinina Ma, Dayu Yan, Gang Li, Zhijun Wang, Huibo Cao, Youguo Shi, and Tao Xiang

In this study, we report the fractional magnetization plateaus and anomalous Hall effect in a newly synthesized kagome intermetallic magnet, $\mathrm{C}{\mathrm{e}}_{3}\mathrm{MgB}{\mathrm{i}}_{5}$. In this compound, the Ce atoms form a distorted kagome lattice, which allows for strong magnetic frus…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144105] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xin Han, Yiqing Hao, Jianfeng Zhang, Zhongchen Xu, Jingyu Yao, Yinina Ma, Dayu Yan, Gang Li, Zhijun Wang, Huibo Cao, Youguo Shi, and Tao Xiang

In this study, we report the fractional magnetization plateaus and anomalous Hall effect in a newly synthesized kagome intermetallic magnet, Ce3MgBi5. In this compound, the Ce atoms form a distorted kagome lattice, which allows for strong magnetic frustration by Ruderman-Kittel-Kasuya-Yosida interac…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144105] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Fractional magnetization plateaus and nontrivial magnetic ground state in the kagome antiferromagnet $\mathrm{C}{\mathrm{e}}_{3}\mathrm{MgB}{\mathrm{i}}_{5}$
item.dc:creator: Xin Han, Yiqing Hao, Jianfeng Zhang, Zhongchen Xu, Jingyu Yao, Yinina Ma, Dayu Yan, Gang Li, Zhijun Wang, Huibo Cao, Youguo Shi, and Tao Xiang

item.title: Topological transition induced by selective random defects on a honeycomb lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Sogen Ikegami, Kiyu Fukui, Shun Okumura, Yasuyuki Kato, and Yukitoshi Motome

We investigate how the spectral and topological properties of electron systems evolve on a lattice that interpolates between the honeycomb and its 1/6-depleted structures through the introduction of selective random defects. We find that in certain parameter regimes, the topological properties of th…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144203] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sogen Ikegami, Kiyu Fukui, Shun Okumura, Yasuyuki Kato, and Yukitoshi Motome

We investigate how the spectral and topological properties of electron systems evolve on a lattice that interpolates between the honeycomb and its 1/6-depleted structures through the introduction of selective random defects. We find that in certain parameter regimes, the topological properties of th…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144203] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026

item.title: Frustration-driven magnetic correlations in the spin-$\frac{5}{2}$ triangular lattice antiferromagnet $\mathrm{RbFe}{({\mathrm{HPO}}_{3})}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): V. Nagpal, Sebin J. Sebastian, Surya P. Patra, S. Shibash, Q.-P. Ding, Y. Furukawa, and R. Nath

A detailed study of the structural and magnetic properties of a spin-$5/2$ triangular lattice antiferromagnet $\mathrm{RbFe}{({\mathrm{HPO}}_{3})}_{2}$ is presented using x-ray diffraction, magnetization, heat capacity, and $^{31}\mathrm{P}$ nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) experiments on a polycrys…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144421] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): V. Nagpal, Sebin J. Sebastian, Surya P. Patra, S. Shibash, Q.-P. Ding, Y. Furukawa, and R. Nath

A detailed study of the structural and magnetic properties of a spin-5/2 triangular lattice antiferromagnet RbFe(HPO3)2 is presented using x-ray diffraction, magnetization, heat capacity, and P31 nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) experiments on a polycrystalline sample. The crystal structure features…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144421] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Frustration-driven magnetic correlations in the spin-$\frac{5}{2}$ triangular lattice antiferromagnet $\mathrm{RbFe}{({\mathrm{HPO}}_{3})}_{2}$

item.title: Raman response in superconducting multiorbital systems with application to nickelates
item.description:
Author(s): Matías Bejas, Jun Zhan, Xianxin Wu, Andreas P. Schnyder, and Andrés Greco

The recent discovery of high-${T}_{c}$ superconductivity in pressurized and thin film nickelates is nowadays one of the most relevant and active topics in solid-state physics. The origin of superconductivity together with the relevance of multiorbital physics are highly discussed issues in this fiel…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144504] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Matías Bejas, Jun Zhan, Xianxin Wu, Andreas P. Schnyder, and Andrés Greco

The recent discovery of high-Tc superconductivity in pressurized and thin film nickelates is nowadays one of the most relevant and active topics in solid-state physics. The origin of superconductivity together with the relevance of multiorbital physics are highly discussed issues in this field. Know…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 144504] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Raman response in superconducting multiorbital systems with application to nickelates

item.title: Resource complexity of symmetry-protected topological phases
item.description:
Author(s): Alberto Giuseppe Catalano, Sven Benjamin Kožić, Gianpaolo Torre, Carola Ciaramelletti, Simone Paganelli, Fabio Franchini, and Salvatore Marco Giampaolo

Topological phases are expected to carry enhanced computational power, stemming from the long-range entanglement that characterizes them. Here we investigate whether this advantage is reflected in quantum nonstabilizerness (often called magic), quantified through stabilizer Rényi entropies. We analy…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155126] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Alberto Giuseppe Catalano, Sven Benjamin Kožić, Gianpaolo Torre, Carola Ciaramelletti, Simone Paganelli, Fabio Franchini, and Salvatore Marco Giampaolo

Topological phases are expected to carry enhanced computational power, stemming from the long-range entanglement that characterizes them. Here we investigate whether this advantage is reflected in quantum nonstabilizerness (often called magic), quantified through stabilizer Rényi entropies. We analy…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 155126] Published Mon Apr 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Resource complexity of symmetry-protected topological phases
item.dc:creator: Alberto Giuseppe Catalano, Sven Benjamin Kožić, Gianpaolo Torre, Carola Ciaramelletti, Simone Paganelli, Fabio Franchini, and Salvatore Marco Giampaolo
